topic: Claude agent replaced $4,200 accountant in 11 minutes
hook_summary: My accountant charged me $4,200 to categorize expenses.

scenes.audio_text: My accountant charged me $4,200 to categorize last year's expenses. One invoice. One year. $4,200.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: medium close-up, 35mm, eye-level [2] SETTING: corner table at a quiet morning cafe, white ceramic espresso cup on the table, open MacBook to his left, crumpled receipt visible near the keyboard [3] LIGHT: warm east-facing window light, 8am golden hour, soft ambient fill from white plaster walls [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: barista tamping espresso behind the counter, steam rising from the machine, a second patron reading a newspaper in soft focus near the window [5] CAMERA + MOOD: locked-off, @daniel leans slightly forward with a flat expression, quiet disbelief energy

scenes.audio_text: She sent me the bill last Tuesday. I opened it, stared at it for about thirty seconds, then closed my laptop.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: over-shoulder wide, 28mm, slight low angle [2] SETTING: open-plan home office, standing desk with a mechanical keyboard, a single potted snake plant in the corner, printed invoice face-down on the desk [3] LIGHT: diffused afternoon light through frosted glass window, 3pm, cool neutral fill from white walls [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: a ceiling fan rotating slowly overhead, notification flicker on a second monitor in the background, ambient street traffic sound implied by passing shadow on the window [5] CAMERA + MOOD: slow 1-foot handheld push toward @daniel's back as he closes the laptop lid, tense stillness, controlled frustration

scenes.audio_text: I spent a full weekend trying to do it myself in Notion. Seventeen tabs open. I quit Sunday night with maybe 40% done.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: medium wide, 50mm, eye-level [2] SETTING: kitchen island at night, Moleskine notebook open with scrawled category names, coffee mug half-empty, a tangled charging cable beside the laptop [3] LIGHT: warm overhead pendant light, 10pm, cool blue spillover from the laptop screen contrasting the warm ambient [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: refrigerator hum implied, a cat walking across the counter behind him in soft focus, condensation visible on the coffee mug [5] CAMERA + MOOD: locked-off, @daniel rubs the back of his neck and exhales, low-energy exhaustion, intimate and relatable

scenes.audio_text: Monday morning I fed every transaction into a Claude agent I built in about two hours. I expected it to get 60% right. It got 97%.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: medium close-up, 35mm, slight high angle [2] SETTING: rooftop terrace at dawn, exposed concrete parapet wall, a single brass travel mug steaming on the ledge, city skyline low in the background [3] LIGHT: early morning blue-hour light transitioning to golden, 6:30am, cool ambient sky fill with warm horizon glow [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: a distant construction crane moving slowly on the skyline, pigeons landing on the ledge behind him, soft wind moving his hair [5] CAMERA + MOOD: slow dolly forward, @daniel's eyes widen slightly mid-sentence — the moment of surprise landing in real-time, quiet revelation energy

scenes.audio_text: Eleven minutes total. I ran it through a Zapier flow to export clean into QuickBooks. Cost me $9 in API calls.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: wide, 24mm, eye-level [2] SETTING: sunlit park bench on a tree-lined path, laptop open on his knees, a paper coffee cup on the bench beside him, earbuds around his neck [3] LIGHT: dappled late-morning sunlight through overhead elm canopy, 10am, bright natural fill with moving leaf shadows on his face [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: a jogger passing behind him on the path, a dog walker crossing the far end of the lane in soft focus, leaves rustling in a breeze [5] CAMERA + MOOD: handheld slow arc right-to-left at waist height, @daniel glances up from the screen with a half-smile, casual confidence, almost bored

scenes.audio_text: I fired her the same week. You're not paying for expertise anymore. You're paying for whoever automated it first.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: tight medium close-up, 50mm, eye-level [2] SETTING: bare garage workshop converted to a small studio, raw wood shelving behind him, a single industrial pendant lamp above, a worn leather notebook closed on a workbench [3] LIGHT: single warm tungsten pendant, late afternoon, hard directional shadows on one side of his face, unlit background receding into shadow [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: dust motes drifting through the lamp beam, a shelf of binders slightly out of focus behind him, faint sound of a power tool implied from outside [5] CAMERA + MOOD: locked-off, @daniel delivers the last line dead to camera with zero expression — flat, final, declarative — then cuts

loop_back_line: You're not paying for expertise anymore. You're paying for whoever automated it first.
